categories.name: أَوْصَافُ الْجِبَالِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
categories.nameEn: Descriptions of the Mountains on the Day of Judgment

items.verseEn: And the mountains will be like wool, fluffed up.
And the mountains will pass on, departing -

items.verse: يَوْمَ تَرْجُفُ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَٱلْجِبَالُ وَكَانَتِ ٱلْجِبَالُ كَثِيبًۭا مَّهِيلًا
items.verseEn: On the Day the earth and the mountains will convulse and the mountains will become a heap of sand pouring down.
And He will leave it [i.e., the earth] a level plain;

categories.name: أَرْبَعَةُ أَلْقَابٍ لِلنَّاسِ حَسَبَ الإِيمَانِ
categories.nameEn: Four Quranic Classifications of People by Faith

items.verse: وَقَالَ رَجُلٌۭ مُّؤْمِنٌۭ مِّنْ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَكْتُمُ إِيمَـٰنَهُۥٓ أَتَقْتُلُونَ رَجُلًا أَن يَقُولَ رَبِّىَ ٱللَّهُ وَقَدْ جَآءَكُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَكُ كَـٰذِبًۭا فَعَلَيْهِ كَذِبُهُۥ ۖ وَإِن يَكُ صَادِقًۭا يُصِبْكُم بَعْضُ ٱلَّذِى يَعِدُكُمْ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى مَنْ هُوَ مُسْرِفٌۭ كَذَّابٌۭ
items.verseEn: And a believing man from the family of Pharaoh who concealed his faith said, "Do you kill a man [merely] because he says, 'My Lord is Allāh' while he has brought you clear proofs from your Lord? And if he should be lying, then upon him is [the consequence of] his lie; but if he should be truthful, there will strike you some of what he promises you. Indeed, Allāh does not guide one who is a transgressor and a liar.

items.verse: فَٱدْعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ ٱلدِّينَ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ
items.verseEn: So invoke Allāh, [being] sincere to Him in religion, although the disbelievers dislike it.

items.verse: وَإِذْ يَقُولُ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌۭ مَّا وَعَدَنَا ٱللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُۥٓ إِلَّا غُرُورًۭا
items.verseEn: And [remember] when the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is disease said, "Allāh and His Messenger did not promise us except delusion,"

items.verse: ثُمَّ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ أَنِ ٱتَّبِعْ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ حَنِيفًۭا ۖ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ
items.verseEn: Then We revealed to you, [O Muḥammad], to follow the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth; and he was not of those who associate with Allāh.

items.verse: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ عَنْهَا لَا تُفَتَّحُ لَهُمْ أَبْوَٰبُ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَلَا يَدْخُلُونَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ حَتَّىٰ يَلِجَ ٱلْجَمَلُ فِى سَمِّ ٱلْخِيَاطِ ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ
items.verseEn: Indeed, those who deny Our verses and are arrogant toward them - the gates of Heaven will not be opened for them, nor will they enter Paradise until a camel enters into the eye of a needle [i.e., never]. And thus do We recompense the criminals.

items.verse: إِنَّا مُرْسِلُوا۟ ٱلنَّاقَةِ فِتْنَةًۭ لَّهُمْ فَٱرْتَقِبْهُمْ وَٱصْطَبِرْ
items.verseEn: Indeed, We are sending the she-camel as trial for them, so watch them and be patient.

items.verse: وَمِنَ ٱلْإِبِلِ ٱثْنَيْنِ وَمِنَ ٱلْبَقَرِ ٱثْنَيْنِ ۗ قُلْ ءَآلذَّكَرَيْنِ حَرَّمَ أَمِ ٱلْأُنثَيَيْنِ أَمَّا ٱشْتَمَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ أَرْحَامُ ٱلْأُنثَيَيْنِ ۖ أَمْ كُنتُمْ شُهَدَآءَ إِذْ وَصَّىٰكُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِهَـٰذَا ۚ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًۭا لِّيُضِلَّ ٱلنَّاسَ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And of the camels, two and of the cattle, two. Say, "Is it the two males He has forbidden or the two females or that which the wombs of the two females contain? Or were you witnesses when Allāh charged you with this? Then who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allāh to mislead the people by [something] other than knowledge? Indeed, Allāh does not guide the wrongdoing people."
And will drink as the drinking of thirsty camels.
And when the trumpet is blown,

items.verse: وَأَخَذَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دِيَـٰرِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And the shriek seized those who had wronged, and they became within their homes [corpses] fallen prone

items.verse: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دَارِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ
items.verseEn: But they denied him, so the earthquake seized them, and they became within their home [corpses] fallen prone.

items.verse: فَعَتَوْا۟ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلصَّـٰعِقَةُ وَهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ
items.verseEn: But they were insolent toward the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they were looking on.

harf.hint: An aromatic herb mentioned alongside the reward of Paradise - 'ryhan' in transliteration
harf.verse: 56:89 - Then [for him is] rest and bounty and a garden of pleasure.

deduction.title: The Curse of Abundant Harvest
deduction.intro: In an ancient land, a certain soul stood among the wealthiest of their community. Twice the blessings of the earth had come to them in consecutive years, a double harvest that should have filled their heart with gratitude. Yet when tragedy struck and everything they possessed was taken in a single night, their response revealed the true nature of their soul. Can you uncover the identity of this figure whose story serves as a warning against disbelief in Allah's blessings?
clues: I was among the most fortunate souls in all the land, blessed with abundant provisions.
Twice the harvest came to me over two years, yet my heart remained ungrateful to my Lord.
I possessed a great garden with vines, olives, and palm trees—a testament to Allah's favor.
When my garden was destroyed, I stood in its ruins with hands outstretched to the heavens, cursing my fate.
I denied that Allah could have caused such loss, even as I crushed my palms in despair.
My punishment was not merely the loss of my garden, but being left utterly helpless in its ruins, unable to invoke any helper.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The owner of the garden in Surah 26
The owner of two gardens from a distant land
A wealthy soul from the city of prosperity
The farmer mentioned in Surah Al-Kahf
A vineyard keeper from the time of Prophet Shu'aib
identity.answer: The owner of the garden in Surah 26

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Loss of a garden destroyed in a single night
The great flood that covered the earth
Being tested with true and false prophets
The trial of being thrown into fire
The trial of being swallowed by the earth
trial.answer: Loss of a garden destroyed in a single night

location.label: Key Element
options: A garden with vines, olives, and palm trees destroyed over two years
A ship built upon a high mountain
The city of a transgressing nation
A well in ancient Midian
The river of paradise
location.answer: A garden with vines, olives, and palm trees destroyed over two years

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Utter helplessness—destroyed with no helper
Resurrection to enter paradise
Becoming a prophet of Allah
Leadership of a great nation
Eternal punishment in the fire

deduction.verse: He said, "Yes, and indeed, you will then be of those near [to me]." (26:42-44)

scramble.hint: A heartfelt supplication of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) asking Allah to make him and his descendants steadfast in prayer.
scramble.arabic: رَبِّ ٱجْعَلْنِى مُقِيمَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِى ۚ رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَآءِ
scramble.verseEn: My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer, and [many] from my descendants. Our Lord, and accept my supplication.
